skill.name: testing-gate
skill.description: Guides a non-blocking testing review that checks whether meaningful tests exist and cover important behavior.
skill.summary: Review test coverage and test quality before moving to code review.
skill.author: DanielPodolsky

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against SKILL.md and were dismissed as false positives. The flagged backticks are Markdown formatting for inline examples and response templates, and the flagged reconnaissance lines are ordinary testing questions, coverage descriptions, or resume guidance. No command execution, weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was found.

content.value_statement: Teams often move to review without knowing whether critical behavior has tests. This skill adds a structured testing gate that encourages useful tests without blocking progress.
actual_capabilities: Asks structured questions about test existence, coverage, and quality.
Classifies testing status as pass or warning without blocking completion.
Provides response templates for missing tests, weak tests, and passing test suites.
Suggests stack-specific testing tools for React, Next.js, APIs, and Python.
Connects testing work to interview and portfolio preparation.
limitations: Does not run tests or inspect coverage reports by itself.
Does not enforce a required coverage percentage.
Relies on the user or agent to identify actual test files.
Provides guidance only and does not replace a full code review.

use_cases.title: Mentor junior developers
use_cases.description: Use the gate to ask clear questions that build testing habits during project reviews.

use_cases.title: Prepare portfolio projects
use_cases.description: Check whether a project has enough tests to support interview discussions and resume claims.

use_cases.title: Standardize review readiness
use_cases.description: Add a lightweight testing checkpoint before a feature moves into code review.

prompt_templates.title: Run the basic testing gate
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing-gate skill to review whether my feature has meaningful tests. Ask the gate questions one at a time.

prompt_templates.title: Review weak test coverage
prompt_templates.scenario: Tests exist but may not cover important behavior
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing-gate skill to assess my test coverage. Focus on happy paths, edge cases, and error states.

prompt_templates.title: Create a test improvement plan
prompt_templates.scenario: A feature has no tests yet
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing-gate skill to identify the first two tests I should add for this feature. Keep the review non-blocking.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare interview talking points
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the testing-gate skill to evaluate my tests and turn the strongest testing decisions into interview talking points.

output_examples.input: I added a login feature and wrote tests for valid login, invalid password, and locked accounts.
output_examples.output: The gate would likely pass. It would note coverage for the happy path, an error path, and an important account state.

output_examples.input: I built a settings page but did not write tests yet.
output_examples.output: The gate would issue a warning, then ask what behavior would be most important to test first.

output_examples.input: I added one test that checks the page renders.
output_examples.output: The gate would warn that the test exists but may be weak, then suggest a behavior-focused improvement.

best_practices: Use the gate after implementation and before code review.
Ask for behavior-based tests instead of implementation details.
Treat warnings as coaching prompts, not delivery blockers.
anti_patterns: Do not use the gate as a substitute for running the test suite.
Do not accept placeholder tests as meaningful coverage.
Do not require full coverage for every trivial change.

faq.question: Does this skill block a feature when tests are missing?
faq.answer: No. It produces warnings and encourages better testing habits.

faq.question: Can it run my tests?
faq.answer: No. It provides review guidance, but another tool must run tests.

faq.question: What kinds of tests does it encourage?
faq.answer: It encourages happy path tests, edge cases, and error state coverage.

faq.question: Is this useful for beginners?
faq.answer: Yes. The questions are designed to teach testing judgment without blocking progress.

faq.question: Does it support specific frameworks?
faq.answer: It includes brief suggestions for React, Next.js, API, and Python testing stacks.

faq.question: Can it help with interviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It prompts users to connect testing decisions to portfolio and interview stories.
